notes: Pyramidal neurons are the canonical cortical glutamatergic excitatory class.
Betz cells are giant layer-V pyramidal (glutamatergic) upper motor neurons.
Generic granule cell (cerebellar/dentate granule cells are glutamatergic).
MSNs are GABAergic projection neurons of striatum; map to GABAergic inhibitory class in BCA.
Cerebellar Purkinje cells are GABAergic output neurons; map to GABAergic class.
VEN has a proper CL term (extratelencephalic-projecting glutamatergic cortical neuron).
Generic 'glia'; BCA splits glia into astrocytes/oligodendrocytes/OPC/microglia, so no single class fits (broad).
Generic 'neuron'; BCA has no single 'neuron' class so mapped to a representative major neuronal class (broad).
Alpha motor neuron has an exact CL term but is a spinal/brainstem cholinergic motor neuron, not a BCA brain cortical major class.
Sensory hair cells of the inner ear; not part of the BCA brain-cell taxonomy.
Dopaminergic/neuromodulatory neurons are not a BCA cortical major class; no clean BCA correspondence.
Fibroblast has an exact CL term but is not a BCA brain major cell class (meningeal/perivascular fibroblasts exist but are not a listed class).
ipRGC has an exact CL term but is a retinal cell, not in BCA brain taxonomy.
Hypothalamic magnocellular neurosecretory cell; not represented in BCA cortical major classes.
Pinealocyte has an exact CL term but is not part of the BCA brain-cell taxonomy.
Pituitary gland cell; endocrine, not part of the BCA brain-cell taxonomy.
SCN pacemaker neuron has an exact CL term; SCN neurons are largely GABAergic but not a BCA cortical major class.
Regional qualifier on inhibitory interneurons; maps to GABAergic class.
Umbrella term for inhibitory neuron diversity; maps to GABAergic class.
Cortical inhibitory interneurons map cleanly to the GABAergic class.
PV+ interneurons are a GABAergic interneuron subtype; CL has no plain 'PV interneuron', closest is inhibitory interneuron.
Striatal interneurons are predominantly GABAergic (plus cholinergic); CL has no plain 'striatal interneuron' parent.
Combined rods+cones; nearest single CL parent is photoreceptor cell. Retinal cells not in BCA taxonomy.
'Hippocampal' is a regional designation; CL hippocampal neuron is a location-based grouping, not a single cell type.
Mixed inhibitory+excitatory grouping; no single CL or single BCA class fits.
'prefrontal' is a brain region, not a cell type; nearest CL is generic cerebral cortex neuron.
Thalamus is a REGION; relay neurons are glutamatergic while reticular nucleus is GABAergic, so no single clean cell type.
Basal forebrain is a REGION; signature cell type is cholinergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
CeA is a brain REGION (predominantly GABAergic); no specific CL term, nearest is regional amygdala neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Dorsal Raphe is a brain REGION; signature cell type is serotonergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Locus Coeruleus is a brain REGION; nearest cell type is its resident noradrenergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Magnocellular pathway is a functional RGC subdivision; CL has no magnocellular RGC term. No BCA class for retina.
LDT is a brain REGION; predominant cell type is cholinergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.

rdoc_label: Neurons of posterior hypothalamic region (tuberomammillary nucleus)
notes: Posterior hypothalamus/TMN is a REGION; signature cell type is histaminergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Pars tuberalis (pituitary) endocrine cell; no specific CL term, and not in BCA brain taxonomy.
Parvocellular pathway is a functional RGC subdivision; CL has no parvocellular RGC term. No BCA class for retina.
PPT is a brain REGION; predominant cell type is cholinergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Substantia Nigra is a brain REGION, not a cell type; nearest CL is the resident SN dopaminergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
TMN is a brain REGION; nearest cell type is its resident histaminergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
VTA is a brain REGION (no CL VTA-neuron term); dominant cell type is midbrain dopaminergic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
REGIONAL designation, not a cell type; only generic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Aggregate of multiple brainstem REGIONS/neuromodulatory systems; no single cell type. No BCA major-class fit.
Calbindin is a calcium-binding PROTEIN marker, not a cell type; no CL cell term. Often marks GABAergic interneurons but ambiguous.
Calretinin (CALB2) is a PROTEIN marker, not a cell type; no CL cell term. Frequently marks a GABAergic interneuron subset but ambiguous.
FUNCTIONAL designation (face-selective response), not an ontological cell type; only generic neuron available.
Hypothalamic REGION (orexin territory), not a cell type; only generic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
Hypothalamic REGIONS (orexin/MCH territory), not a cell type; only generic neuron. No BCA major-class fit.
FUNCTIONAL designation, not an ontological cell type; only generic neuron available.
'Ribbon synapse' is a synaptic structure, NOT a cell type; no CL cell term and no BCA class.
